diagrams.title: Raster Grid Pixel Selection
diagrams.description: Shows a 10x10 grid with an ideal line from (2,2) to (8,7). Demonstrates how Bresenham's algorithm selects which pixels (squares) to illuminate to approximate the line.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how discrete pixels approximate a continuous line and understand the midpoint decision concept.

diagrams.title: Bresenham Decision Process
diagrams.description: Illustrates the decision-making at each step showing the two candidate pixels (East and Northeast) and the midpoint between them.
diagrams.purpose: Help visualize why the decision parameter determines which pixel is closer to the ideal line.

diagrams.title: DDA vs Bresenham Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing both algorithms plotting the same line endpoints, demonstrating similar but not identical pixel selections.
diagrams.purpose: Understand the differences between algorithms and why Bresenham is more efficient.

diagrams.title: Aliasing Effect
diagrams.description: Shows a line drawn without anti-aliasing (jagged edges) vs with anti-aliasing (smooth edges).
diagrams.purpose: Understand the aliasing problem and how anti-aliasing techniques produce smoother lines.

visuals.title: Parallel Line Algorithms
visuals.description: Visual diagram for parallel line algorithms
